sections.label: Section 1.4.199c
sections.original: Huius patrem vidisse me , patruum tuum .
sections.translation: That I have seen his father , your uncle .

sections.label: Section 1.4.200
sections.original: Nam quod ego huic nunc subito exitio remedium inveniam miser ?
sections.translation: How am I , wretch that I am , now to find a remedy for this sudden misfortune ? But if it should be my fortune , Phanium , to be torn away from you , life would cease to be desirable .

sections.label: Section 1.4.202b
sections.original: Ergo istaec quum ita sint , Antipho ,
sections.translation: Therefore , Antipho , since matters are thus , the more need have you to be on your guard ; fortune helps the brave .

sections.label: Section 1.4.204
sections.translation: I am not myself .

sections.label: Section 1.4.204b
sections.original: Atqui opus est nunc quummaxime ut sis , Antipho ;
sections.translation: But just now it is especially necessary you should be so , Antipho ;

sections.label: Section 1.4.205
sections.original: Nam si senserit te timidum pater esse , arbitrabitur
sections.translation: for if your father perceives that you are alarmed , he will think that you have been guilty of some fault .

sections.label: Section 1.4.206b
sections.translation: That ’ s true .

sections.label: Section 1.4.206c
sections.translation: I can not change .

sections.label: Section 1.4.207
sections.original: Quid faceres , si gravius aliud tibi nunc faciendum foret ?
sections.translation: What would you do , if now something else still more difficult had to be done by you ?

sections.label: Section 1.4.208
sections.original: Quum hoc non possum , illud minus possem .
sections.translation: As I am not equal to this , I should be still less so to the other .

sections.label: Section 1.4.208b
sections.translation: This is doing nothing at all , Phaedria , let ’ s be gone ; why do we waste our time here to no purpose . I shall be off .

sections.label: Section 1.4.209b
sections.translation: And I too .

sections.label: Section 1.4.209c
sections.translation: Pray , now ,

sections.label: Section 1.4.210
sections.translation: if I assume an air , will that do ? ( He endeavors to assume another air .)

sections.label: Section 1.4.210b
sections.translation: You are trifling .

sections.label: Section 1.4.210c
sections.translation: Look at my countenance — there ’ s for you . ( Assuming a different air .) Will that do ?

sections.label: Section 1.4.211b
sections.translation: No .

sections.label: Section 1.4.211c
sections.translation: Well , will this ? ( Assuming another air .)

sections.label: Section 1.4.211d
sections.translation: Pretty well .

sections.label: Section 1.4.211e
sections.translation: Well then , this ? ( Assuming a still bolder air .)

sections.label: Section 1.4.211f
sections.translation: That ’ s just the thing . There now , keep to that , and answer him word for word , like for like ; don ’ t let him , in his anger , disconcert you with his blustering words .

sections.label: Section 1.4.213b
sections.translation: I understand .

sections.label: Section 1.4.214
sections.original: Vi coactum te esse invitum , lege , iudicio : tenes ?
sections.translation: Say that you were forced against your will by law , by sentence of the court ; do you take me ?

sections.label: Section 1.4.215
sections.original: Sed quis hic est senex quem video in ultima platea ?
sections.translation: ( Looking earnestly in one direction .) But who is the old man that I see at the end of the street ?

sections.label: Section 1.4.215b
sections.translation: ’ Tis he himself . I can not stand it . ( Going .)

sections.label: Section 1.4.216b
sections.original: Ah , quid agis ? quo abis , Antipho ?
sections.translation: Oh ! What are you about ? Whither are you going , Antipho ? Stop , I tell you .

sections.label: Section 1.4.217b
sections.original: Egomet me novi et peccatum meumr :
sections.translation: I know my own self and my offense ; to your management I trust Phanium and my own existence . ( Exit hastily .)

sections.label: Section 1.4.219
sections.translation: Geta , what ’ s to be done now ?

sections.label: Section 1.4.219b
sections.translation: You will just hear some harsh language :

sections.label: Section 1.4.220
sections.original: Ego plectar pendens , nisi quid me fefellerit .
sections.translation: I shall be trussed up and trounced , if I am not somewhat mistaken . But what we were just now advising Antipho to do , the same we must do ourselves , Phaedria .

sections.label: Section 1.4.223
sections.original: Aufer mihi oportet : quin tu quod faciam impera .
sections.translation: Away with your musts ; rather do you command me what I am to do .

sections.label: Section 1.4.224
sections.original: Meministine olim ut fuerit vestra oratio
sections.translation: Do you remember what were your words formerly

sections.label: Section 1.4.225
sections.original: In re incipienda ad defendendam noxiam ;
sections.translation: on our entering upon this project , with the view of protecting yourselves from ill consequences — that their cause was just , clear , unanswerable , and most righteous ?

sections.label: Section 1.4.227
sections.translation: I remember it .

sections.label: Section 1.4.227b
sections.original: Hem , nunc ipsa est opus ea , aut , si quid potest ,
sections.translation: Well then , now there ’ s need of that plea , or of one still better and more plausible , if such there can be .

sections.label: Section 1.4.228b
sections.translation: I ’ ll use my best endeavors .

sections.label: Section 1.4.229
sections.original: Nunc prior adito tu : ego in subsidiis hic ero
sections.translation: Do you then accost him first ; I ’ ll be here

sections.label: Section 1.4.230
sections.translation: in re-serve , [*] by way of reinforcement , if you give ground at all .

sections.label: Section 1.4.230b
sections.translation: Very well .

sections.label: Section 2.1.231
sections.original: Itane tandem uxorem duxit Antipho iniussu meo ?
sections.translation: And is it possible that Antipho has taken a wife without my consent ? and that no authority of mine — but let alone authority [*] — no displeasure of mine , at all events , has he been in dread of ? To have no sense of shame ! O audacious conduct ! O Geta , rare adviser !

sections.label: Section 2.1.234b
sections.translation: Just brought in at last .

sections.label: Section 2.1.234c
sections.original: Quid mihi dicent , aut quam causam reperient ,
sections.translation: What will they say to me , or what excuse will they find ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.235
sections.translation: I wonder much .

sections.label: Section 2.1.235b
sections.translation: Why , I ’ ve found that out already ; do think of something else .

sections.label: Section 2.1.235c
sections.translation: Will he be saying this to me : I did it against my will ; the law compelled me ? I hear you , and admit it .

sections.label: Section 2.1.236b
sections.translation: Well said !

sections.label: Section 2.1.237
sections.original: Verum scientem , tacitum , causam tradere adversariis ;
sections.translation: But knowingly , in silence , to give up the cause to his adversaries — did the law oblige him to do that as well ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.238b
sections.translation: That is a hard blow .

sections.label: Section 2.1.238c
sections.translation: I ’ ll clear that up ; let me alone for that .

sections.label: Section 2.1.239
sections.original: Incertum est quid agam , quia praeter spem atque incredibile hoc mihi obtigit :
sections.translation: It is a matter of doubt what I am to do ; for beyond expectation , and quite past all belief , has this befallen me .

sections.label: Section 2.1.240
sections.original: Ita sum irritatus animum ut nequeam ad cogitandum instituere :
sections.translation: So enraged am I , that I can not compose my mind to think upon it . Wherefore it is the duty of all persons , when affairs are the most prosperous , [*] then in especial to reflect within themselves in what way they are to endure adversity . Returning from abroad , let him always picture to himself dangers and losses , either offenses committed by a son , or the death of his wife , or the sickness of a daughter ,—

sections.label: Section 2.1.245
sections.original: Communia esse haec ; nequid horum unquam accidat animo novum :
sections.translation: that these things are the common lot , so that no one of them may ever come as a surprise upon his feelings . Whatever falls out beyond his hopes , all that he must look upon as so much gain .

sections.label: Section 2.1.247
sections.original: Phaedria , incredibile est quantum herum anteco sapientia .
sections.translation: O Phaedria , it is incredible how much I surpass my master in wisdom . All my misfortunes have been already calculated upon by me , upon my master coming home . I must grind at the mill , be beaten , wear fetters ,

sections.label: Section 2.1.250
sections.original: Opus ruri faciendum : horum nihil quicquam accidet animo novum .
sections.translation: be set to work in the fields ; not one individual thing of these will happen unexpected by my mind . Whatever falls out beyond my expectations , all that I shall look upon as so much gain . But why do you hesitate to accost him , and soften him at the outset with fair words ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.253
sections.original: Phaedriam mei fratris video filium mihi ire obviam .
sections.translation: I see Phaedria , my brother ’ s son , coming toward me .

sections.label: Section 2.1.254
sections.translation: My uncle , welcome !

sections.label: Section 2.1.254b
sections.translation: Greetings to you ; but where is Antipho ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.255
sections.translation: That you have arrived in safety —

sections.label: Section 2.1.255b
sections.translation: I believe it ; answer my question .

sections.label: Section 2.1.256
sections.original: Valet : hic est : sed satin omnia ex sententia ?
sections.translation: He is well ; he ’ s close at hand ; but is every thing quite to your wishes ?
